title: T-081 — fix migrate.ts 017-rbac.sql checksum mismatch; replay 023+024 cleanly (FT-021)
prompt:
# T-081 — fix migrate.ts 017-rbac.sql checksum mismatch; replay 023+024 cleanly (FT-021)

PRD: `docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §4.2, §5
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`
Original carry-forward ID: **FT-021** (from `_meta/audits/2026-05-07-foundation-final-carry-forward-backlog.md`)
Severity: **P0** (S effort)

## Goal
Rebase 017-rbac.sql canonical checksum, add --reconcile-checksums recovery flag, replay 023/024 via the runner.

## Implementation contract
1. Re-derive the canonical SHA-256 of the current 017-rbac.sql content and update schema_migrations.
2. Add `--reconcile-checksums` flag that, for a list of named migrations, updates the stored checksum to match disk after a human review.
3. Replay 023 and 024 via the runner; assert success.
4. Runbook documents when/how to use the flag and the safety guardrails (must run with explicit --confirm and an audit log entry).

## Files (scope)
- scripts/migrate.ts [modify]
- scripts/__tests__/migrate-checksum.test.ts [create]
- docs/runbooks/migrate-checksum-recovery.md [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the repo state, when `pnpm tsx scripts/migrate.ts --reconcile-checksums 017-rbac.sql --confirm` is run, then schema_migrations.checksum for 017-rbac.sql matches the SHA-256 of the on-disk file.
2. Given checksums are reconciled, when `pnpm tsx scripts/migrate.ts` is re-run, then it applies 023, 024 (and any subsequent) cleanly without manual psql.
3. Given the unit-test pack, when run, then the runner aborts when checksum drift is detected without --reconcile-checksums + --confirm.

## Test strategy
- RED: write scripts/__tests__/migrate-checksum.test.ts asserting both the drift-abort and reconcile-flag paths before implementation.
- Run `pnpm test migrate-checksum`.

## Risk red lines
- P0 deploy blocker — do not merge without GREEN evidence on staging
- Do not auto-reconcile without explicit --confirm.
- Do not silently mutate migration file contents.
- Do not skip the audit-log entry on reconcile.

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED-first) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.

pipeline_inputs.description: Fix the 017-rbac.sql checksum mismatch in migrate.ts so every future migration applies without manual psql workaround; replay 023 and 024 cleanly.
pipeline_inputs.details: Both T-013 and T-039 had to bypass `pnpm tsx scripts/migrate.ts` and apply migrations via direct psql + manual schema_migrations insert because the runner aborts on 017-rbac.sql checksum mismatch. Rebase the canonical checksum, add a `--reconcile-checksums` flag for one-time recovery, and document the procedure.
out_of_scope: Do not change the migration runner's transaction-boundary semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Given the repo state, when `pnpm tsx scripts/migrate.ts --reconcile-checksums 017-rbac.sql --confirm` is run, then schema_migrations.checksum for 017-rbac.sql matches the SHA-256 of the on-disk file.
Given checksums are reconciled, when `pnpm tsx scripts/migrate.ts` is re-run, then it applies 023, 024 (and any subsequent) cleanly without manual psql.
Given the unit-test pack, when run, then the runner aborts when checksum drift is detected without --reconcile-checksums + --confirm.
test_strategy: RED: write scripts/__tests__/migrate-checksum.test.ts asserting both the drift-abort and reconcile-flag paths before implementation.
